source: Luca Pacioli — De Divina Proportione (Public Domain, Gutenberg 25326); Leonardo da Vinci — Notebooks (Public Domain, Gutenberg 5000); general art theory.

golden_ratio.definition: The golden ratio φ ≈ 1.618... Cannot be expressed as a ratio of integers. Cannot be precisely written. It can only be approached, asymptotically, through successive approximations.
golden_ratio.pacioli_fragment: Without mathematics there is no art. The divine proportion is the mirror in which all beauty studies itself.
golden_ratio.puzzle_application: The pentagon is constructed with compass and straightedge — the ancient Euclidean method. Its diagonals divide each other in the golden ratio.

steps: Mark any point on the circumference — this is vertex 1
Use compass to step the radius around the circle five times
Connect the five points: a regular pentagon
pentagon_construction.note: The construction is inevitable. Once begun with correct tools, it cannot fail.

copy_vs_original.problem: A perfect reproduction is not the original. The original contains the irreproducible: the specific moment of its making, the imperfections that are signatures.
copy_vs_original.tarkovskij_note: Andrei Tarkovsky (the director) walks ahead in the gallery — always at the same distance, always facing away. He represents the impossible original: the artistic vision that can be approached but never owned.
copy_vs_original.the_proportion_description: A golden compass with no hinge. It can measure anything but cannot be closed. The proportion is always open.

mirror_mechanics.clean_break: If psychological weight = 0: the mirror shatters into a perfect pentagon. A golden compass with no hinge emerges from the fragments.
mirror_mechanics.chaotic_break: If psychological weight > 0: each fragment reflects a different version of the protagonist carrying something they should have left behind. No simulacrum appears.
mirror_mechanics.implication: The gallery sector cannot be completed by someone who has accumulated weight. The proportion requires empty hands.

backward_walking.puzzle_note: The door exists only in the reflection. To pass through a door that exists only in the mirror, one must walk backward — facing the mirror while moving away from it.
backward_walking.philosophical_note: To see clearly, sometimes one must stop facing forward.

llm_prompts.gallery_weight_0: The gallery is precise. Every reflection is accurate. The protagonist can see clearly.
llm_prompts.gallery_weight_3plus: The mirrors are distorted by weight. Every reflection adds another layer of what was taken.
